source_jobs.title: Sr. Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEON REDWOOD

Neon Redwood is a data services consulting company, working on cutting-edge AI and data-driven solutions. We are a team of passionate engineers and data experts, and we are currently looking for a Senior Data Engineer to join our team and help us develop and expand our data infrastructure and analytics capabilities.



THE ROLE

We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Engineer with a strong background in data engineering and a passion for working with large-scale data sets. Help us develop and expand our data infrastructure and analytics capabilities.

The ideal candidate will have at least 5 years of professional experience and a solid understanding of Python, BigQuery, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP) or similar technologies. This full-time role will involve working closely with our CTO and other team members to design, develop, and maintain data pipelines, ETL processes, and data warehousing solutions.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate with the CTO and other team members to design, develop, and maintain data pipelines and ETL processes.

 - Write clean, efficient, and maintainable code in Python and other relevant technologies.

 - Implement and optimize data storage and processing solutions using BigQuery and Google Cloud Platform (GCP).

 - Ensure data quality and integrity through proper data validation and monitoring techniques.

 - Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to improve overall code quality.

 - Stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and technologies to ensure our data infrastructure remains competitive.

 - Assist in the development and launch of new data-driven tools and products.

 - Mentor and guide junior engineers, fostering a culture of continuous learning and improvement.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.

 - 5+ years of professional data engineering experience.

 - Strong proficiency in Python, BigQuery, and Google Cloud Platform (GCP) or equivalent technologies.

 - Experience with data pipeline and ETL process design and development.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently or as part of a team.

 - Strong communication, collaboration, and leadership skills.

 - Passion for working with large-scale data sets and staying current with industry trends.


ADDITIONAL SKILLS (NICE TO HAVE)

 - Experience with other data processing technologies and platforms (e.g., Apache Beam, Dataflow, Hadoop, Spark).

 - Experience with data visualization tools and libraries (e.g., Tableau, Looker, D3.js).

 - Knowledge of machine learning and AI concepts.

 - Experience with real-time data processing and streaming technologies (e.g., Kafka, Pub/Sub).


BENEFITS

 - Fully covered Health Insurance for employees and their eligible dependents

 - Fully covered Vision & Dental for employees and their eligible dependents

 - Unlimited Time Off

 - Company 401k plan with employer contributions

 - Commuter Benefits Program

source_jobs.title: Venture Services Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEON REDWOOD

Neon Redwood is a data services consulting company, working on cutting-edge AI and working on data-driven solutions. We are a team of passionate engineers and data experts, and we are currently looking for an Application Engineer to join our team and help us develop and expand our tools and products.




THE ROLE

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Venture Services Associate with at least one year of experience in fund accounting or related financial services. In this role, you will work closely with a portfolio of venture capital firms to support fund operations, financial reporting, and investor relations. This is a unique opportunity to deepen your expertise in venture fund accounting and gain firsthand experience in the day-to-day financial operations of VC funds.



You will play a key role in reviewing fund financials, coordinating with fund administrators and auditors, and helping ensure our clients' financial and investor reporting needs are met with accuracy and timeliness. The ideal candidate will bring strong accounting knowledge, a proactive mindset, and the ability to thrive in a fast-paced, client-facing environment.



Responsibilities:



Fund Accounting & Financial Analysis:

 - Review capital account statements, financial statements, and investor allocations prepared by fund administrators to ensure accuracy

 - Reconcile capital calls, distributions, and management fees across internal records and fund administrator reports.

 - Assist in preparing internal financial models and performing variance and flux analysis across funds and periods.

 - Track key financial metrics such as unfunded commitments, NAV, and fund cash flow activity.
   

Fund Operations & Administration:

 - Collaborate with fund administrators to oversee fund bookkeeping, investment entries, and expense allocations.

 - Monitor fund activities and support the review of quarterly and annual financial reporting packages.

 - Help manage the coordination of audits, tax filings, and compliance deliverables with third-party service providers.
   

Client & Investor Support:

 - Serve as a point of contact for clients, providing responsive support on accounting and reporting inquiries.

 - Assist with investor reporting workflows, including preparing capital call and distribution notices and managing document delivery.

 - Support investor onboarding and manage investor data in portals and internal systems.
   

General Support:

 - Maintain organized records and documentation for each client, including accounting files, investor correspondence, and compliance documents.

 - Support ad hoc financial analyses, special projects, and process documentation.



Key Skills & Attributes for Success:

 - Fund Accounting Knowledge: Understanding of fund structures, LP/GP/Management Company accounting, capital calls/distributions, and investment valuations

 - Attention to Detail: Accuracy in reviewing financial statements, cash flow data, and investor allocations is critical.

 - Analytical Thinking: Ability to interpret and analyze financial data to support internal and external reporting.

 - Strong Communication: Clear, professional written and verbal communication when working with clients, investors, administrators, and internal teams.

 - Proactive & Organized: Ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines with minimal supervision.

 - Team-Oriented: Works collaboratively across teams and is open to feedback and continuous improvement.

Qualifications & Requirements:

 - Bachelor's degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.

 - Minimum 1 year of experience in fund accounting, public accounting, or VC/PE financial operations.

 - Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills (e.g., data analysis, pivot tables, financial modeling)

 - Experience working with fund administrators or in a venture capital/private equity environment is a plus.

 - High degree of professionalism, accountability, and client service orie

source_jobs.title: Venture Services Coordinator
source_jobs.description:
Job Description

We are seeking a Venture Operations Coordinator to join Neon Redwood’s Venture Services team, which supports a portfolio of venture capital firms and their operating companies. In this role, you will manage the day-to-day operational tasks that keep our clients running smoothly. This is an ideal opportunity for someone with 0-2 years of operations and administrative experience. Venture Capital ecosystem experience is a plus.

This is a fast-paced, hands-on position where no two days look the same. You will have direct visibility into how venture capital firms operate and grow, and your work will directly enable that growth. We invest in our people: this role offers significant room for advancement, mentorship, and continuous learning, with a clear path to take on greater responsibility as you develop. The ideal candidate is process- and detail-oriented, proactive, and thrives in a dynamic, client-facing environment.



Responsibilities:

Client & Account Management:

 - Manage and prioritize the day-to-day operational tasks for an assigned portfolio of venture capital firm and operating company clients.

 - Serve as a responsive, professional point of contact for client requests, keeping each account organized and on track.

 - Track deliverables and deadlines across multiple clients simultaneously, ensuring nothing falls through the cracks.

 - Maintain accurate, well-organized records and documentation for each client, including correspondence, filings, and compliance files.

Compliance & Filings Monitoring:

 - Monitor and maintain a compliance calendar capturing each entity’s filing due dates across jurisdictions.

 - Track annual report and biennial statement deadlines and confirm acceptance of filings.

 - Assist with the preparation and submission of corporate documents, including entity formations, amendments, and dissolutions.

 - Navigate state and local agency processes to confirm filing status, resolve holds, and reach the appropriate point of contact.

Vendor & State Agency Coordination:

 - Contact and coordinate with vendors, registered agents, and third-party service providers to ensure timely filings and uninterrupted service.

 - Communicate with secretaries of state and other state agencies to keep client entities in good standing.

 - Monitor state-level changes to forms, fees, due dates, and submission requirements, and flag any changes that affect client compliance.

 - Reconcile vendor invoices and service confirmations against client records.

General Operations Support:

 - Support venture capital firms and their operating companies with ad hoc operational requests and projects.

 - Help document and improve internal processes as the team scales.

 - Collaborate with internal teams to deliver accurate, timely results for clients.



Key Skills & Attributes for Success:

 - Highly Organized: Able to juggle multiple clients, deadlines, and tasks with minimal supervision.

 - Attention to Detail: Accuracy in tracking filings, deadlines, and client records is critical.

 - Proactive Mindset: Anticipates needs, takes initiative, and follows through without being asked.

 - Strong Communication: Clear, professional written and verbal communication with clients, vendors, agencies, and internal teams.

 - Patient & Persistent: Stays calm and determined through long hold times, bureaucratic processes, and difficult or unhelpful contacts. Follows up until the issue is resolved.

 - Comfortable in a Fast-Paced Environment: Thrives in a dynamic, high-volume, client-facing capacity and stays composed under deadline pressure.

 - Eager to Learn: Excited to grow within venture operations and take on increasing responsibility over time.



Qualifications & Requirements:

 - Bachelor’s degree (any field; Business, Finance, or a related area is a plus).

 - 0–2 years of operations, administrative, or coordination experience.

 - Strong proficiency with Excel or Google Sheets and general comfort learning

source_jobs.title: Platform Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Backend Engineer



The Company

Neon Redwood is a data services consulting company working on cutting-edge AI and data-driven solutions. We are a team of passionate engineers and data experts, and we are currently looking for a Backend Engineer to join us in developing and expanding our data infrastructure and analytics capabilities.

The Role

We are seeking an experienced Backend Engineer with a background with some knowledge of front end development and a passion for building data-heavy web applications

The ideal candidate will have at least 4 years of professional experience and a strong understanding of LLMs, TypeScript and Amazon Web Services (AWS) or similar technologies. This full-time role involves working closely with our CTO and team members to design, develop, and maintain apis, web applications, and LLM integrations.

Responsibilities

* Implement multi-step agentic workflows using LLMs

* Collaborate with the CTO and team members to design, develop, and maintain systems and applications

* Write clean, efficient, and maintainable code in TypeScript and related technologies

* Implement and optimize data storage and processing solutions using PostgreSQL and AWS

* Develop tooling to support resilient agentic workflows

* Stay up to date with industry trends and technologies to keep our infrastructure competitive

* Assist in the development and launch of new data-driven tools and products

Requirements

* Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent experience

* 4+ years of professional full-stack or backend engineering experience

* Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work independently or collaboratively

* Strong communication skills and a desire to grow into leadership roles

* Experience with Asana or similar project management tools

* Understanding of API design and development

* Solid proficiency in TypeScript

* Familiarity with Next.js

Nice to Have

* Experience with LangChain or similar tools in production

* Knowledge of machine learning and AI concepts

* Experience with Prisma

* Familiarity with SST and/or Pulumi

* Experience integrating LLMs into production systems

source_jobs.title: Application Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT NEON REDWOOD

Neon Redwood is a data services consulting company, working on cutting-edge AI and working on data-driven solutions. We are a team of passionate engineers and data experts, and we are currently looking for an Application Engineer to join our team and help us develop and expand our tools and products.




THE ROLE

We are seeking an experienced Application Engineer with a strong background in software development and a passion for creating web apps. The ideal candidate will have at least 3 years of professional experience and a solid understanding of TypeScript, React, Next.js, Google Cloud (or similar), and PostgreSQL. This role will involve working closely with our team members to build innovative tools and products.



RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Write clean, efficient, and maintainable code in TypeScript, React, Next.js, and other relevant technologies.

 - Work on both the backend and frontend of a modern web application.

 - Implement and optimize cloud-based infrastructure using Google Cloud or similar platforms.

 - Work with PostgreSQL or similar databases to store and manage data effectively.

 - Participate in code reviews and provide constructive feedback to improve overall code quality.

 - Stay up-to-date with the latest industry trends and technologies to ensure our products remain competitive.




REQUIREMENTS

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field, or equivalent work experience.

 - 3+ years of professional software development experience.

 - Strong proficiency in TypeScript, React, Next.js, and PostgreSQL or similar technologies.

 - Experience with Google Cloud or similar cloud platforms.

 - Sass/Css experience.

 - Excellent problem-solving skills and the ability to work independently or as part of a team.

 - Strong communication and collaboration skills.

 - Experience with Asana or similar project management tools.



Additional Skills (Nice to Have)

 - API design experience.

 - Experience with testing frameworks.

 - Well-formed opinions about what makes software great.

 - Experience deploying web applications in a production environment.

 - Experience with integrating LLMs into applications

source_jobs.title: Senior IT Manager
source_jobs.description:
Senior IT Manager

About Neon Redwood

Neon Redwood is a technology and IT services consulting company, delivering managed IT, cybersecurity, and cloud operations for venture capital, private equity, family office, and life-sciences clients. We are a team of passionate engineers and IT professionals, and we are currently looking for a Senior IT Manager to expand our managed IT and security practice across our internal team and our client portfolio.



The Role

We are seeking a hands-on IT Manager with strong experience across managed IT, cloud infrastructure, and endpoint security. As part of the technical backbone of our day-to-day operations: keeping environments secure, compliant, and well-documented for Neon Redwood and the client organizations we serve.

This full-time, on-site role (San Francisco Bay Area) reports to our Senior Director of IT and works alongside a dynamic IT team. You'll spend your day administering cloud and identity systems, supporting users, implementing security controls, and helping onboard new clients and employees. It's a great fit for someone who likes solving real technical problems across a variety of environments rather than sitting in strategy meetings.

Responsibilities

 - Provide day-to-day IT support and operations for Neon Redwood and a portfolio of client organizations: troubleshooting, ticket resolution, and keeping systems running.

 - Administer cloud and identity infrastructure across AWS (VPN, backup/recovery), Google Workspace (GAM), SSO platforms, and device management.

 - Implement and maintain security controls and endpoint protection; help enforce security policies (WISP, acceptable-use/AI, DR, incident response) and assist with incident response when issues arise.

 - Endpoint and device lifecycle: provisioning, onboarding/offboarding, hardware procurement, and inventory.

 - Support compliance and data-retention work for regulated clients, including SEC document retention and cloud archival migrations.

 - New-client onboarding and transitions: environment setup, migrations, and documentation.

 - Help evaluate and test vendor tools; run side-by-side comparisons before the team commits to a platform.

 - Support physical IT and office build-outs: networking (Cisco Meraki/UniFi), AV and conference rooms, badging, and physical security (Verkada).

 - Help enable AI tooling (Claude, ChatGPT, NotebookLM) with appropriate security and compliance guardrails.



Requirements

 - 5+ years of hands-on IT experience, including systems administration or IT engineering in a multi-client or multi-entity cross platform environment.

 - Strong hands-on skills across cloud and identity: AWS, Google Workspace, Okta, Duo, and device management.

 - Solid endpoint security and IT operations fundamentals; comfortable implementing controls and following established security policies.

 - White glove support for end users and resolving technical issues calmly across many concurrent priorities.

 - Strong documentation habits and clear communication.

 - Bachelor's degree, or equivalent practical experience.


Additional Skills (Nice to Have)

 - Experience in an MSP or fractional-IT model serving VC, PE, family office, or biotech clients.

 - Experience supporting regulated industries (financial services, life sciences) and their compliance requirements.

 - Hands-on with networking and physical-security hardware (Meraki, UniFi, Verkada).

 - Familiarity with backup/DR tooling and archival/compliance platforms (Box, Smarsh).

 - Experience with AI-tool enablement (Claude, ChatGPT, Google Workspace Studio).
   

Benefits

 - Fully covered Health Insurance for employees and their eligible dependents

 - Fully covered Vision & Dental for employees and their eligible dependents

 - Unlimited Time Off

 - Company 401k plan with employer contributions

 - Commuter Benefits Program

source_jobs.title: Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
